comments.author: namankumawat13
comments.body:
After a recent vulnerability report, I updated my React project to force an upgraded version of glob by adding this to my package.json:
`"resolutions": {
  "glob": "^10.5.0"
}`

However, after running a fresh install and rebuilding, the build now fails with the following error:
`[webpack-cli] TypeError: pify(...).bind is not a function
    at Object.<anonymous> (/Users/{MY_USER}/node_modules/globby/index.js:8:33)`

It appears that globby is still expecting an older version of glob and breaks when forced to use glob@10, since the API changed.

Is there a recommended workaround to keep the vulnerability fix without breaking tooling that depends on older versions of glob?

comments.author: mitar
comments.body:
[nyc](https://www.npmjs.com/package/nyc) also does not work with v10:

```
TypeError [ERR_INVALID_ARG_TYPE]: The "original" argument must be of type function. Received an instance of Object
```

It looks like v9 would work. Could you please release a fixed version of v9?

comments.author: magrinj
comments.body: The PR https://github.com/github/advisory-database/pull/6429 has been merged, so I think it should be fine now !
